mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting near-term corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0 percent over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal savings (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust household balance sheet strength and durable demand, which jointly elevate equity risk premiums and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 15 basis points per month over a 24-month out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects sustained capacity utilization and pricing power, which historically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as earnings momentum peaks.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 60% of late-cycle quarters where INDPRO rises for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 2.5 percent annualized, inflation expectations embed into longer-term discount rates, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annualized inflation rate exceeds 2.5 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses the 2.5 percent threshold over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) directly measures aggregate economic expansion, which drives immediate upward repricing of equity cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days following each data release.
failure_condition: The sign of GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth fails to predict the sign of 5-day forward SPX returns with accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) and a simultaneously increasing unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates structural labor market friction and rising wage costs, which erode corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a pre-recession tightening environment, rapid increases in the federal funds rate force aggressive discount rate hikes, which systematically depress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of pre-recession tightening episodes identified by a 2-year rolling yield curve inversion signal over a 10-year test period.
